entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop grown for its edible seeds.

entities.name: ICGV 91114
entities.description: A new variety of groundnut, also known as Anantha Jyoti, characterized by higher fodder yields and drought tolerance.

entities.name: FODDER YIELD
entities.description: The quantity of plant material produced that can be used as livestock feed.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain growth and productivity during periods of water scarcity.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A condition of deficient rainfall resulting in a shortage of water.

entities.name: ANANTAPUR
entities.description: A district in Andhra Pradesh, India, where the new groundnut variety was introduced.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, an organization that introduced the new groundnut variety.

entities.name: IFAD
entities.description: The International Fund for Agricultural Development, an organization that provided financial support to ICRISAT.

entities.name: FARMER PARTICIPATORY ON-FARM VARIETAL SELECTION APPROACH
entities.description: A method of crop improvement involving farmers in the selection of varieties on their own farms.

entities.name: PROGRAMME FOR FARMER-PARTICIPATORY IMPROVEMENT OF GRAIN LEGUMES IN RAINFED ASIA
entities.description: A grant-funded initiative (2002-2007) to improve grain legumes in rainfed Asian regions.

relationships.target: FARMER PARTICIPATORY ON-FARM VARIETAL SELECTION APPROACH
PROGRAMME FOR FARMER-PARTICIPATORY IMPROVEMENT OF GRAIN LEGUMES IN RAINFED ASIA
